preamble: Spirantization or lenition of voiced aspirated stops like [bʱ] into fricatives like [v] is a common evolutionary path in several language families, often occurring through intermediate stages of spirantization and de-aspiration.

examples.note: Development of PIE *bʰ often went through an intermediate [f/v] stage in Latin (e.g. *bhrātēr > frater, though PIE *bʰ often became [f] word-initially and [b/v] medially).
Dialectal weakening in modern Indo-Aryan languages like Hindi or Punjabi sometimes shows [bʱ] being lenited to [v] in rapid speech.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
